title: medicago hispida subsp. lappacea
intro: Medicago hispida subsp. lappacea, also known as purple medic or lapp's medic, is a perennial herb belonging to the legume family Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits associated with Medicago hispida subsp. lappacea include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and digestive problems.
